get.summary:
QueryWriteLog searches the audit log for config change events.
All filter parameters are optional — omit a filter to match all values.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Filter by tenant ID (UUID). If omitted, returns entries across all tenants.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Filter by actor (JWT subject). If omitted, matches all actors.

parameters.name: fieldPath
parameters.description: Filter by field path. If omitted, matches all fields.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Filter entries created at or after this time. If omitted, no lower bound.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: Filter entries created at or before this time. If omitted, no upper bound.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50, max 100.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous QueryWriteLogResponse.

get.summary: ListSchemas returns all schemas, ordered by creation time (newest first).

parameters.name: nameFilter
parameters.description: Filter schemas by name prefix. Currently reserved for future use.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50, max 100.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous ListSchemasResponse.

post.summary: CreateSchema creates a new schema with an initial draft version (v1).

ImportSchema creates a schema (or new version) from YAML.
Full-replace semantics: the YAML defines the complete field set.
Returns AlreadyExists if the imported fields are identical to the latest version.

get.summary: GetSchema retrieves a schema by ID, optionally at a specific version.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Schema ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Schema version to retrieve. If omitted, returns the latest version.

delete.summary: DeleteSchema permanently deletes a schema and all its versions. Cascades to tenants.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Schema ID (UUID). Cascades to all versions, fields, and associated tenants.

patch.summary: UpdateSchema creates a new draft version by merging field changes with the latest version.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Schema ID (UUID).

get.summary: ExportSchema serializes a schema version to YAML.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Schema ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Schema version to export. If omitted, exports the latest version.

post.summary:
PublishSchema marks a schema version as published and immutable.
Only published versions can be assigned to tenants.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Schema ID (UUID).

get.summary: ListTenants returns tenants, optionally filtered by schema.

parameters.name: schemaId
parameters.description: Filter by schema ID (UUID). If omitted, returns tenants across all schemas.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50, max 100.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous ListTenantsResponse.

post.summary: CreateTenant creates a new tenant assigned to a published schema version.

get.summary: GetTenant retrieves a tenant by ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

delete.summary: DeleteTenant permanently deletes a tenant and all its configuration data.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID). Cascades to all config versions, values, and field locks.

patch.summary: UpdateTenant updates a tenant's name or upgrades its schema version.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

get.summary: GetConfig returns the full resolved configuration for a tenant.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Config version to retrieve. If omitted, returns the latest version.

parameters.name: includeDescriptions
parameters.description:
When true, includes value-level descriptions in the response.
This bypasses the Redis cache and reads directly from the database.

get.summary: ExportConfig serializes a tenant's configuration to YAML.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Config version to export. If omitted, exports the latest version.

get.summary: GetField returns a single configuration value by field path.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: fieldPath
parameters.description: Dot-separated field path (e.g. "payments.fee").

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Config version to read from. If omitted, reads from the latest version.

parameters.name: includeDescription
parameters.description:
When true, includes the value-level description.
This bypasses the Redis cache and reads directly from the database.

put.summary: SetField sets a single configuration value, creating a new config version.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: fieldPath
parameters.description: Dot-separated field path (e.g. "payments.fee").

post.summary: ImportConfig applies configuration values from YAML.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

post.summary:
GetFields returns multiple configuration values by field paths.
Fields that don't exist are silently omitted from the response.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

post.summary: SetFields sets multiple configuration values in a single config version.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

get.summary:
Subscribe opens a server-streaming connection that pushes ConfigChange events
whenever the tenant's configuration is modified. The stream remains open until
the client disconnects or the server shuts down.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID) to subscribe to.

parameters.name: fieldPaths
parameters.description:
Field paths to filter on. If empty, receives changes for all fields.
Changes to fields not in this list are silently dropped.

get.summary: ListFieldLocks returns all active field locks for a tenant.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

post.summary: LockField prevents a field from being modified.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

delete.summary: UnlockField removes a field lock, allowing modifications again.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: fieldPath
parameters.description: Dot-separated field path to unlock.

get.summary:
GetUnusedFields returns field paths that have not been read since the given time.
Useful for identifying configuration fields that may be safe to deprecate.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
Fields not read since this time are considered unused.
Required — there is no "all time" default to avoid expensive full scans.

get.summary: GetTenantUsage returns aggregated read statistics for all fields of a tenant.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start of the time range. If omitted, includes all historical data.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End of the time range. If omitted, includes up to the current time.

get.summary: GetFieldUsage returns aggregated read statistics for a specific field.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: fieldPath
parameters.description: Dot-separated field path to query.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start of the time range. If omitted, includes all historical data.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End of the time range. If omitted, includes up to the current time.

get.summary: ListVersions returns config version history for a tenant (newest first).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50, max 100.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous ListVersionsResponse.

get.summary: GetVersion retrieves metadata for a specific config version.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version number to retrieve.

post.summary:
RollbackToVersion creates a new version with the same values as the target version.
This does not delete intermediate versions — it creates a new version that
copies the target's values.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant ID (UUID).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description:
The target version to rollback to. The full config at this version
is copied into a new version (the current version number + 1).
The target version itself is not modified.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description:
Description for the new rollback version. Defaults to
"Rollback to version N" if omitted.

get.summary: GetServerVersion returns the server's build version and commit hash.

version.description: Config version to read from. If omitted, reads from the latest version.

includeDescriptions.description:
When true, includes value-level descriptions.
This bypasses the Redis cache and reads directly from the database.

description.description: Description for the new config version created by the import.

value.description: The typed value. Omit to set the field to null.

expectedChecksum.description:
Optimistic concurrency control: the checksum from a previous GetField/GetConfig
response. If provided and the field's current checksum doesn't match, the
request fails with ABORTED. This prevents lost updates when multiple actors
modify the same field concurrently.

description.description: Version-level description explaining why this change was made.

valueDescription.description:
Value-level description explaining what this specific value means.
Retrievable via include_description in read requests.

updates.description:
Field updates to apply. All updates are applied atomically in a single
config version. If any update fails validation (checksum, field lock),
no changes are committed.

description.description: Version-level description explaining why these changes were made.

lockedValues.description:
For enum fields: lock only these specific values. If empty, the
entire field is locked regardless of value.

version.description:
The version number to publish. Must be an existing draft version.
Once published, the version is immutable.

versionDescription.description: Description of what changed in this version.

fields.description:
Fields to add or modify. Existing fields not listed here are carried
forward unchanged from the latest version.

removeFields.description: Dot-separated paths of fields to remove from the new version.

name.description: New name for the tenant. Must be a valid slug if provided.

schemaVersion.description:
Upgrade to a new schema version. The new version must belong to the
same schema and must be published.

actor.description: The actor who performed the action (from JWT subject).

fieldPath.description: The field that was changed. Present for set_field actions.

oldValue.description: The previous value. Present for set_field actions.

newValue.description:
The new value. Present for set_field actions.
For rollback actions, contains the target version (e.g. "v2").

configVersion.description: The config version number created by this action.

createdAt.description: When the audit entry was created.

v1AuditEntry.description:
AuditEntry represents a write event recorded in the audit log.
Every config mutation (SetField, SetFields, RollbackToVersion) creates
one or more audit entries atomically with the config change.

tenantId.description: The tenant this config belongs to (UUID).

version.description: The version number this config was resolved at.

values.description: All configuration values at this version.

v1Config.description: Config represents the full resolved configuration for a tenant at a specific version.

version.description: The new config version number created by this change.

oldValue.description: The previous value. Absent if field was newly created or was null.

newValue.description: The new value. Absent if field was set to null.

changedBy.description: The actor who made the change.

v1ConfigChange.description:
ConfigChange represents a real-time change event pushed to subscribers
via the Subscribe streaming RPC.

value.description: The typed value. Absent when the field value is null.

checksum.description:
Checksum of the value (xxHash). Used for optimistic concurrency control
in SetField/SetFields via expected_checksum.

description.description:
Human-readable description explaining this specific value.
Only populated when include_description(s) is true in the request.

v1ConfigValue.description: ConfigValue represents a single configuration value at a point in time.

tenantId.description: The tenant this version belongs to (UUID).

description.description: Description of what changed in this version.

createdBy.description: The actor who created this version (from JWT subject, or "unknown" if auth is disabled).

v1ConfigVersion.description:
ConfigVersion represents a point-in-time snapshot of configuration changes.
Each write operation (SetField, SetFields, RollbackToVersion) creates a new
config version. Versions store only the changed fields (delta storage) — the
full config at any version is the union of all deltas up to that version.

name.description:
Unique name for the schema. Must be a valid slug: lowercase alphanumeric
and hyphens, 1-63 characters (e.g. "payment-config", "settlement-rules").

description.description: Human-readable description of the schema's purpose.

fields.description: Initial field definitions for version 1. At least one field is required.

schema.description: The created schema with version 1 (draft, unpublished).

name.description:
Unique name for the tenant. Must be a valid slug: lowercase alphanumeric
and hyphens, 1-63 characters (e.g. "acme-corp", "tenant-42").

schemaId.description: The schema to assign to this tenant (UUID).

schemaVersion.description: The schema version to use. Must be a published version.

yamlContent.description:
YAML-encoded schema (syntax v1). Includes schema name, description,
version, and all field definitions with OAS-style constraint naming.
Server-generated fields (id, checksum, published, created_at) are excluded.

description.description: Human-readable description of the external documentation.

v1ExternalDocs.title:
ExternalDocs links to external documentation.
OAS: External Documentation Object

regex.description:
Regular expression pattern the value must match.
Applies to string-typed fields. Uses RE2 syntax.

enumValues.description:
Allowed values. If non-empty, the value must be one of these.
Applies to any field type.

jsonSchema.description:
JSON Schema document for structural validation of json-typed fields.
Encoded as a JSON string.

v1FieldConstraints.description:
FieldConstraints defines validation rules for a schema field.
Which constraints apply depends on the field's type — see FieldType docs.

value.description: The example value, encoded as a string matching the field type.

summary.description: Short description of what this example demonstrates.

v1FieldExample.description: FieldExample represents a named example value for a schema field.

tenantId.description: The tenant this lock applies to (UUID).

fieldPath.description: The dot-separated field path that is locked.

lockedValues.description:
For enum fields: the specific subset of values that are locked
(not editable by admin). If empty, the entire field is locked.

v1FieldLock.description:
FieldLock prevents a configuration field from being modified by non-superadmin users.
Superadmins bypass all field locks.

v1FieldType.description:
FieldType enumerates the supported configuration value types.
Each type maps to a specific field in the TypedValue oneof.

 - FIELD_TYPE_INT: Integer value. Encoded as a decimal string (e.g. "42", "-1").
Supports minimum/maximum constraints on the numeric value.
 - FIELD_TYPE_STRING: Free-form string value.
Supports minimum/maximum constraints on string length, pattern (regex),
and enum constraints.
 - FIELD_TYPE_TIME: Timestamp value. Encoded as an RFC 3339 string (e.g. "2025-01-15T09:30:00Z").
 - FIELD_TYPE_DURATION: Duration value. Encoded as a Go-style duration string (e.g. "24h", "30m", "500ms").
Supports minimum/maximum constraints on the duration in seconds.
 - FIELD_TYPE_URL: URL value. Must be a valid absolute URL.
 - FIELD_TYPE_JSON: JSON value. Stored as a JSON-encoded string.
Supports json_schema constraint for structural validation.
 - FIELD_TYPE_NUMBER: Floating-point number value. Encoded as a decimal string (e.g. "3.14", "0.025").
Supports minimum/maximum constraints on the numeric value.
 - FIELD_TYPE_BOOL: Boolean value. Encoded as "true" or "false".

value.description: The typed value. Omit to set the field to null.

expectedChecksum.description:
Optimistic concurrency control checksum for this specific field.
See SetFieldRequest.expected_checksum for details.

valueDescription.description: Value-level description for this specific field.

v1FieldUpdate.description: FieldUpdate represents a single field change within a SetFields batch.

config.description: The full resolved configuration at the requested version.

value.description: The configuration value. Returns NOT_FOUND if the field has no value set.

stats.description: Aggregated usage statistics across the queried time range.

values.description: The requested values. Fields that don't exist are omitted (not an error).

fieldStats.description: Per-field usage statistics, ordered by field path.

fieldPaths.description: Dot-separated paths of fields with no reads since the specified time.

configVersion.description: The config version created by the import.

v1ImportMode.description:
ImportMode controls how imported values interact with existing config.

 - IMPORT_MODE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified defaults to merge behavior.
 - IMPORT_MODE_MERGE: Merge: update fields from YAML that differ, keep runtime overrides
for fields not in the YAML.
 - IMPORT_MODE_REPLACE: Replace: full replace — all fields from YAML are set, fields not in YAML
are not carried forward. Runtime overrides are wiped.
 - IMPORT_MODE_DEFAULTS: Defaults: only set fields that have no value yet. Fields that already have
a value are skipped regardless of the YAML content.

yamlContent.description:
YAML-encoded schema (syntax v1). Must include `syntax`, `name`, and `fields`.

Import uses full-replace semantics:
- If no schema with this name exists: creates a new schema with version 1.
- If a schema exists and fields differ from latest: creates the next version.
- If a schema exists and fields are identical: returns AlreadyExists error.

Imported versions are created as drafts (unpublished) unless auto_publish is true.
The `version` field in the YAML is informational — the server assigns
the next version number automatically.

autoPublish.description:
When true, the imported version is automatically published after creation.
If the schema already exists and fields are identical (AlreadyExists), this has no effect.

schema.description: The created (or existing, on AlreadyExists) schema version.

locks.description: All active field locks for the tenant.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.
Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

versions.description: Config versions, ordered by version number descending (newest first).

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

entries.description: Audit entries matching the filters, ordered by created_at descending.

nextPageToken.description: Token for the next page. Empty if no more results.

configVersion.description: The newly created config version containing the rolled-back values.

name.description:
Unique name for this schema. Must be a valid slug: lowercase alphanumeric
characters and hyphens, 1-63 characters, matching [a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?.

description.description: Human-readable description of the schema's purpose.

version.description: Schema version number (monotonically increasing, starting at 1).

parentVersion.description: The version this was derived from. Absent for the initial version (v1).

versionDescription.description: Description of what changed in this version.

checksum.description:
Deterministic checksum of the field definitions (type, constraints, path).
Used for change detection on import.

published.description:
Whether this version is published. Only published versions can be
assigned to tenants. Published versions are immutable.

fields.description: The fields defined in this schema version.

info.description: Optional schema metadata: ownership, contact, labels.

v1Schema.description:
Schema represents a configuration schema template.
Schemas define the allowed fields and their types for tenant configurations.
Each schema is versioned — updates create new immutable versions.

v1SchemaContact.title:
SchemaContact contains contact information for a schema owner.
OAS: Contact Object

path.description:
Dot-separated hierarchical path (e.g. "payments.settlement.window").
Must be unique within a schema version.

type.description: The value type for this field. Controls validation behavior.

constraints.description:
Validation constraints. Optional — when absent, any value of the
correct type is accepted.

nullable.description: Whether this field accepts empty/null values.

deprecated.description:
Whether this field is deprecated. Deprecated fields are still readable
but clients should migrate to redirect_to if set.

redirectTo.description: When deprecated, reads of this field can be redirected to this path.

defaultValue.description: Default value for this field, encoded as a string matching the field type.

description.description: Human-readable description of the field's purpose.

title.title:
Human-friendly display name (e.g. "Fee Rate" for path "payments.fee_rate").
OAS: title

example.title:
Single example value, encoded as a string matching the field type.
OAS: example

examples.title:
Named examples with optional summary. Key is example name.
OAS: examples

externalDocs.title:
Link to external documentation for this field.
OAS: externalDocs

tags.title:
Tags for grouping and categorization beyond the dot-prefix hierarchy.
OAS: x-tags

format.title:
Semantic format hint within the base type (e.g. "email", "semver", "percentage").
Informational — not enforced by validation.
OAS: format

readOnly.title:
Whether this field is system-managed and not user-editable.
OAS: readOnly

writeOnce.description: Whether this field can only be set once and becomes immutable after.

sensitive.description: Whether this field's value should be masked in logs and UI.

v1SchemaField.description: SchemaField defines a single field within a configuration schema.

title.title:
Human-friendly display title for the schema.
OAS: info.title

author.description: Schema owner identifier (person, team, or service).

contact.title:
Contact information for the schema owner.
OAS: info.contact

labels.description: Key-value labels for filtering and categorization.

v1SchemaInfo.title:
SchemaInfo contains optional metadata about a schema's ownership and context.
OAS: Info Object

change.description: A configuration change event. Delivered via Redis Pub/Sub internally.

name.description:
Unique name for this tenant. Must be a valid slug: lowercase alphanumeric
characters and hyphens, 1-63 characters, matching [a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?.

schemaId.description: The schema this tenant's configuration is based on (UUID).

schemaVersion.description:
The specific schema version assigned to this tenant.
Must reference a published schema version.

updatedAt.description: When the tenant was last updated (name or schema version change).

v1Tenant.description:
Tenant represents an organization or entity that has its own configuration
based on an assigned schema version.

urlValue.description: URL value (must be a valid absolute URL).

v1TypedValue.description:
TypedValue holds a configuration value with its native type.
An unset oneof (no field present) represents a null value.

readCount.description: Total number of reads across the queried time range.

lastReadBy.description: The last actor who read this field (if tracked).

lastReadAt.description: When this field was last read (if tracked).

v1UsageStats.description: UsageStats represents aggregated read usage statistics for a config field.
